title: Ilona Marhele

infobox.value: This biographical article relating to Latvian athletics is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Ilona_Marhele&action=edit).

(born 5 April 1986) is a Latvian [long-distance runner](/wiki/Long-distance_runner "Long-distance runner") who specialises in the [marathon](/wiki/Marathon "Marathon"). She competed in the [women's marathon](/wiki/Athletics_at_the_2016_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_marathon "Athletics at the 2016 Summer Olympics – Women's marathon") event at the [2016 Summer Olympics](/wiki/2016_Summer_Olympics "2016 Summer Olympics") in Brazil, finishing in 61st place with a time of 2:41:02.[[1]](#cite_note-Bio-1) Her P.R. for the event is 2.37:29, a time she accomplished in the year 2019.

["Ilona Marhele"](https://web.archive.org/web/20160815041631/https://www.rio2016.com/en/athlete/ilona-marhele). *Rio 2016*. Archived from [the original](https://www.rio2016.com/en/athlete/ilona-marhele) on 15 August 2016. Retrieved 15 August 2016.

outgoing_links.target_title: Athletics at the 2016 Summer Olympics – Women's marathon

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Latvian long-distance runner

Ilona Marhele

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Ilona Marhele running the marathon at the 2018 European Athletics Championships | |
| Personal information | |
| Born | (1986-04-05) 5 April 1986 (age 39)  [Alūksne](/wiki/Al%C5%ABksne "Alūksne"), [Latvian SSR](/wiki/Latvian_SSR "Latvian SSR"), Soviet Union |
| Sport | |
| Sport | [Track and field](/wiki/Track_and_field "Track and field") |
| Event | [Marathon](/wiki/Marathon "Marathon") |

**Ilona Marhele** (born 5 April 1986) is a Latvian [long-distance runner](/wiki/Long-distance_runner "Long-distance runner") who specialises in the [marathon](/wiki/Marathon "Marathon"). She competed in the [women's marathon](/wiki/Athletics_at_the_2016_Summer_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_marathon "Athletics at the 2016 Summer Olympics – Women's marathon") event at the [2016 Summer Olympics](/wiki/2016_Summer_Olympics "2016 Summer Olympics") in Brazil, finishing in 61st place with a time of 2:41:02.[[1]](#cite_note-Bio-1) Her P.R. for the event is 2.37:29, a time she accomplished in the year 2019.

## References

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ilona_Marhele&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: References")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-Bio_1-0)** ["Ilona Marhele"](https://web.archive.org/web/20160815041631/https://www.rio2016.com/en/athlete/ilona-marhele). *Rio 2016*. Archived from [the original](https://www.rio2016.com/en/athlete/ilona-marhele) on 15 August 2016. Retrieved 15 August 2016.
